page: Microsoft Azure E32pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the EPDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache per core, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. The instance is equipped with 208.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.5 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 1229 GB local SSD storage volume with Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are present on this hardware configuration.

Benchmark results place the E32pds_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The Ampere processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1822.89 and a multi-core score of 57247.25, alongside a Geekbench multi-core score of 13049.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 2231.95 GB/sec, dropping to 170.75 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write bandwidth of 77.64 GB/sec. Database operations are supported by strong Redis SET throughput at 41.96 million operations per second and a PassMark database score of 21161.56.

While the instance demonstrates solid capabilities for general database workloads, web serving with 32.4 GB/sec static throughput, and software compilation, it exhibits significant performance limitations in large-scale machine learning. Large language model inference benchmarks for a 70B parameter model show poor performance, yielding only 3.6 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 2.43 tokens/sec for text generation. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers, and web servers, rather than heavy AI or deep learning workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32pds_v5 is an ARM-based, memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the EPDSv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz. The hardware profile includes 208.0 GB of RAM and 1229 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13049.0 and a PassMark database score of 21161.56. While highly efficient for memory-intensive tasks, database operations, and web serving, the instance exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM inference tasks. It is best suited for relational databases, caching systems, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 208 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32pds_v5 is a 32-core ARM-based instance with 208 GB RAM and 1229 GB local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering balanced memory density, dedicated physical cores, and high-capacity local storage for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
208 GB of system memory providing 6.5 GB of RAM per core
1229 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage support
Strong database performance with 41.96 million Redis SET operations per second
Poorly suited for large-scale LLM inference workloads
Ideal for relational databases, caching layers, and web serving
